questions.q: The main reason for international trade is?
opts: (A) To increase national income
(B) To reduce national income
(C) To increase foreign exchange earnings
(D) To reduce foreign exchange earnings
questions.explanation: International trade increases the earning of foreign exchange for a country which can be used to buy goods and services from other countries.

opts: (A) International Monetary Fund
(B) World Trade Organization
(C) World Bank
(D) International Labour Organization
questions.explanation: GATT is an acronym for General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade which is now a part of the World Trade Organization (WTO).

questions.q: The main objectives of GATT are?
opts: (A) Reduce tariffs and non-tariff barriers to trade
(B) Raise tariffs and non-tariff barriers to trade
(C) Reduce and abolish quantitative restrictions
(D) Raise and increase quantitative restrictions
questions.explanation: GATT aims to reduce tariffs and non-tariff barriers to facilitate international trade.
WTO was established in 1995 as a result of the Uruguay Round of GATT negotiations.

questions.q: Which of the following is a feature of the New International Division of Labour?
opts: (A) Industrialization of developing countries
(B) Transfer of industries from developed to developing countries
(C) Transfer of industries from developing to developed countries
(D) No transfer of industries
questions.explanation: The New International Division of Labour refers to the transfer of industries from developed countries to developing countries.

questions.q: What is the significance of the New International Division of Labour?
opts: (A) To reduce employment opportunities in the developed countries
(B) To increase employment opportunities in the developed countries
(C) To reduce employment opportunities in the developing countries
(D) To increase employment opportunities in the developing countries
questions.explanation: The New International Division of Labour has increased employment opportunities in the developing countries.

questions.q: The core countries are characterized by?
opts: (A) Low per capita income and low skill levels
(B) High per capita income and high skill levels
(C) Medium per capita income and medium skill levels
(D) Low per capita income and high skill levels
questions.explanation: The core countries are characterized by high per capita income and high skill levels.

questions.q: The core countries are also known as?
opts: (A) Developed countries
(B) Developing countries
(C) Newly industrializing countries
(D) Least developed countries
questions.explanation: The core countries are also known as developed countries.

questions.q: The periphery countries are characterized by?
opts: (A) High per capita income and high skill levels
(B) Low per capita income and low skill levels
(C) Medium per capita income and medium skill levels
(D) Low per capita income and high skill levels
questions.explanation: The periphery countries are characterized by low per capita income and low skill levels.

questions.q: The periphery countries are also known as?
opts: (A) Developed countries
(B) Developing countries
(C) Newly industrializing countries
(D) Least developed countries
questions.explanation: The periphery countries are also known as developing countries.

questions.q: The primary function of the Core is?
opts: (A) To produce raw materials for the Periphery
(B) To produce manufactured goods for the Periphery
(C) To produce finished goods for the Core
(D) To produce raw materials and manufactured goods for the Core
questions.explanation: The primary function of the Core is to produce manufactured goods for the Periphery.

questions.q: The primary function of the Periphery is?
opts: (A) To produce manufactured goods for the Core
(B) To produce raw materials for the Core
(C) To produce finished goods for the Periphery
(D) To produce raw materials and manufactured goods for the Periphery
questions.explanation: The primary function of the Periphery is to produce raw materials for the Core.

questions.q: The New International Division of Labour has resulted in?
opts: (A) Increased employment opportunities in the Core
(B) Reduced employment opportunities in the Periphery
(C) Increased employment opportunities in the Periphery
(D) Reduced employment opportunities in the Core
questions.explanation: The New International Division of Labour has increased employment opportunities in the Periphery.

questions.q: The major advantages of the New International Division of Labour are?
opts: (A) Increased employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness
(B) Reduced employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness
(C) Increased employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness
(D) Reduced employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness
questions.explanation: The major advantages of the New International Division of Labour are increased employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness.

questions.q: The major disadvantages of the New International Division of Labour are?
opts: (A) Increased employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness
(B) Reduced employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness
(C) Increased employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness
(D) Reduced employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness
questions.explanation: The major disadvantages of the New International Division of Labour are reduced employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness.

questions.q: The major differences between the old and new international division of labour are?
opts: (A) Increased employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness
(B) Reduced employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness
(C) Increased employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness
(D) Reduced employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness
questions.explanation: The major differences between the old and new international division of labour are increased employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness.

questions.q: The concept of 'Global Value Chain' is related to?
opts: (A) International Division of Labour
(B) New International Division of Labour
(C) International Trade
(D) Globalization
questions.explanation: The concept of 'Global Value Chain' is related to the New International Division of Labour.

questions.q: The Global Value Chain is characterized by?
opts: (A) Increased employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness
(B) Reduced employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness
(C) Increased employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness
(D) Reduced employment opportunities, increased foreign exchange earnings, and increased competitiveness
questions.explanation: The Global Value Chain is characterized by increased employment opportunities, reduced foreign exchange earnings, and reduced competitiveness.
